care.summary: A classic echeveria forming tight rosettes of plump, spoon-shaped blue-green leaves with pinkish tips.
care.light: Bright direct to indirect light; needs 4-6 hours of sun to maintain compact rosette form
care.water: Water thoroughly when soil dries out; every 1-2 weeks in summer, reduce in winter
care.soil: Very well-draining cactus/succulent mix with perlite and coarse sand
care.humidity: Low; prefers dry air, avoid humidity
care.fertilizer: Dilute succulent fertilizer once in early spring and again mid-summer
common_issues: Leggy, open rosette from insufficient light
Root rot from overwatering or poor drainage
tips: Give as much light as possible for tight, colorful rosettes
Water at the soil level, not on leaves, to prevent rot
Remove dead lower leaves to prevent pests
Propagate easily from leaf cuttings placed on dry soil
